old_claim.note: Pottery provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because herbalism is plant-use medicinal knowledge; vessels can support preparation or storage but are not a prerequisite for the practice.

source_shape.source_locator: Definition and discussion of phytotherapy and traditional herbalism as use of plant-derived medications and empirical medicinal herbs.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Britannica describes herbal medicine around plant-derived treatments and traditional medicinal-herb knowledge rather than ceramic-vessel technology.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the retained foraging/botany boundary and gives no basis for making pottery a direct enabling prerequisite; pottery is at most a downstream preparation or storage context.

ontology_before: The graph treated a storage/preparation vessel technology as a prerequisite for plant medicinal knowledge.
ontology_after: Herbalism remains grounded in plant knowledge and ritual/empirical practice; vessel technologies can appear on narrower preparation nodes.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-10-random-sample-quality-fixes keeps this direct edge absent.
would_reject_if: The herbalism node were narrowed to ceramic-vessel extraction or storage systems.
A source showed early herbalism as scoped here required pottery rather than direct plant use, chewing, poultices, teas in non-pottery containers, or other preparations.
A future split moved pottery onto a narrower herbal_medicine_preparation node.
